title: stapelia verrucosa var. pulchra
intro: Stapelia verrucosa var. pulchra is a rare and unique succulent plant species, commonly known as the 'Pulchra Carrion Flower' or 'Carrion Blossom', due to its distinctive appearance and strong odor, resembling the scent of rotting flesh. Traditionally, the plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant's extracts have been found to have potential health benefits, such as wound healing and antimicrobial activity, as well as being used to treat skin conditions and promote tissue repair. In herbal preparations, the plant's extracts are often used in topical creams, ointments, and salves, as well as being incorporated into tinctures and infusions.
